identity.short_name: Sum of mixed-boundary Pascal-grid entries for n=27 mod 1000

problem_spec.summary: Define an n×n grid by the Pascal recurrence a(i,j)=a(i-1,j)+a(i,j-1) with a mixed boundary: top row is 1..n while the left column is constant 1. Find S(n)=sum of all entries and compute S(27) mod 1000.
givens: n is a positive integer (instance uses n=27).

parameter(s).name: boundary_profile
parameter(s).purpose: initializes the recurrence on the top/left borders

invariant(s).name: pascal_grid_binomial_closed_form
invariant(s).statement: With top row a(1,j)=j and left column a(i,1)=1, the unique solution is a(i,j)=C(i+j-1,i)=C(i+j-1,j-1), since it matches the boundary and satisfies Pascal's identity.

invariant(s).name: iterated_hockey_stick_for_total_sum
invariant(s).statement: S(n)=\sum_{i=1}^n\sum_{j=1}^n C(i+j-1,i) reduces via two hockey-stick applications to S(n)=C(2n+1,n)-(n+1).

invariant(s).name: uniqueness_from_boundary_and_recurrence
invariant(s).statement: The boundary values and recurrence determine all interior entries uniquely by forward filling, hence S(n) is uniquely determined.

essential_assumption(s): Binomial coefficients use the standard integer definition and standard identities.
The recurrence is applied over integers (not modulo) before the final modular reduction.
uniqueness_certificate(s): The recurrence with the specified boundary conditions has a unique solution array. The closed form gives a unique integer S(n), and reducing mod 1000 produces a unique residue.

technique(s).name: derive_closed_form_entry_via_pascal_identity
technique(s).purpose: guess and verify a binomial closed form for a(i,j) using boundary matching and Pascal's identity

technique(s).name: evaluate_total_sum_by_double_hockey_stick
technique(s).purpose: evaluate S(n) by summing over j then over i using hockey-stick identities

technique(s).name: substitute_and_reduce_mod
technique(s).purpose: compute S(27) from the closed form and reduce to a residue mod 1000

theorem(s).name: Pascal_identity
theorem(s).role: C(m,r)=C(m-1,r)+C(m-1,r-1) validates the recurrence for binomial closed forms

theorem(s).name: uniqueness_of_grid_recurrence_solution
theorem(s).role: boundary plus local recurrence determines the grid uniquely

closest_first_principle_concept_applied: interpret recurrence values through binomial identities, then evaluate totals by standard summation identities

critical_trick(s): Recognize the mixed boundary forces the simple binomial form a(i,j)=C(i+j-1,i).
Use hockey-stick twice to collapse the double sum to S(n)=C(2n+1,n)-(n+1).

library(s).name: math
library(s).reason: provides exact integer binomial coefficients via comb for large values

library(s).name: itertools
library(s).reason: optional dynamic-programming verification of the recurrence table for n=27

task(s).name: compute_binomial_mod_1000
task(s).reason: compute comb(55,27) exactly and reduce mod 1000 to get the final residue

task(s).name: dynamic_program_check_mod_1000
task(s).reason: compute the grid values mod 1000 by recurrence and sum them to confirm the same residue
